what_it_does: Defines window.AcExplore, the React UI for the Explore surface. It renders a live product surface (Canvas — a real-estate listing card whose parts are selectable hotspots: card/button/price/badge/stat), lets the user select an element directly on canvas, then calls CV_EXPLORE.variations to generate distinct style directions, shows them as a strip of live-rendered chips with like/dislike voting and a focus, applies the focused recipe to the selected element in place, surfaces a taste profile + iteration buttons (More like these / Bolder / Quieter / Refine this), and promotes the keeper into the system via CV_EXPLORE.promote. It also shows a persisted gallery of previously promoted components. Includes a renderEl helper that paints a recipe onto each element kind.
observations: Header states 'Selection-on-canvas is the core gesture' — pick an element on the live surface, restyle it right there, steer by taste, then promote.
All exploration logic is delegated to window.CV_EXPLORE (variations/styleFor/promote/tasteProfile/gallery/onGallery/forget); this file is the view layer.

Canvas renders a concrete listing card (Property Wizard / 14 Eclipse Boulevard / $1.84m / 360 tour / 23.8k views) with each region as an ex3-hot hotspot that calls onSelect and stops propagation so nested clicks target the right element.
The focused recipe's styleFor() output is applied to the selected element on the canvas (appliedStyle), so the change previews in context.
Voting: like adds to liked and removes from disliked and sets focus; dislike adds to disliked and removes from liked; taste profile recomputed from liked via useMemo.

Iteration buttons pass intent phrases ('lean into the liked directions and refine', 'go bolder, diverge hard', 'quieter, restrained, minimal', 'subtle variations around this exact direction' with a seed) into generate().
Promote sets a 'in your system' done state noting whether it was staged for review and pointing to Under the hood › Registries.
Gallery is reactive via onGallery + a force reducer; items render their stored recipe live and can be forgotten.
Errors from generation route to window.dsaToast; busy state drives 'Exploring…/Sketching…' affordances.
Uses window.AcKit.StateDot and CvIcon/ViShape shared primitives; reuses .ex2-* classes alongside .ex3-* (the two Explore styling generations coexist).
standouts: Selection-on-canvas: the user restyles a real fragment of the actual product surface in place, not an abstract swatch — the focused variant applies live to the clicked element.
The full divergent→steer→converge→promote loop is expressed as direct manipulation, with the promoted result landing back in the system registries.
Taste profile + a convergence bar make the steering visible and immediate.
conventions: Pure view over the CV_EXPLORE engine — generation/promotion/persistence live in the engine, matching the project's separation (recipes are data, markup renders them).
Shared primitives (AcKit, CvIcon, ViShape) and the prefixed class namespaces (ex3-/ex2-).
window.AcExplore global registration consumed by Atlas.jsx's AcBody.
concerns: Reuses both .ex2-* and .ex3-* class families in one component; from this file alone it's unclear which Explore generation is canonical, a minor coherence smell.
Canvas default styles for some hotspots are empty objects ({}), so an unselected price/badge/stat relies entirely on CSS classes — fine, but the def map's intent is only partly populated.
notes: The Explore view — restyle a real element directly on a live listing card, vote to steer, watch the taste profile converge, then promote the keeper into the system; all heavy lifting is in CV_EXPLORE (explore-engine.js).
questions: How does on-canvas selection coordinate with Inspect mode (⌘E) referenced in the hint — does CV_PICK/CV_MODE let the user select arbitrary on-screen elements beyond the demo card?
Why do both .ex2-* and .ex3-* style families appear — is .ex2 (gallery-of-variants) deprecated in favor of .ex3 (selection-on-canvas)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header describes select-on-canvas → generate directions → like/dislike → iterate → promote, and the component implements exactly that flow over the CV_EXPLORE engine.
apparent_themes: Direct manipulation of the real product surface
Divergent generation steered by taste into a single-sourced component
Make-it-real promotion back into the registries
View as a thin projection over an engine
intention_signals: Trying to make restyling feel like pointing at the actual product and saying 'make this look different', then converging by taste to a keeper that becomes a real component — turning an open creative act into a concrete addition to the system.
novelty: Restyling by selecting parts of a live product card in place and previewing AI-generated directions directly on the clicked element, with the chosen one promotable into the architecture.
contribution: Contributes the Explore interface: the selectable on-canvas product surface, the variant strip with voting/focus, the taste/iteration controls, the promote action, and the promoted-components gallery view — the human-facing half of the explore-to-component loop.
summary_for_embedding: window.AcExplore — the React Explore surface where a user selects a part of a live product listing card, generates distinct AI style directions via CV_EXPLORE, votes/steers them with a visible taste profile, applies the focused recipe in place, and promotes the keeper into the system, alongside a persisted gallery of promoted components.
intention_for_embedding: To let a person restyle a real element of the product by direct selection and converge by taste to a keeper that becomes a real component in the architecture.
